project_objectives: Generate a comprehensive Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document for an e-commerce platform.
Define core e-commerce functionalities including product management, shopping cart, checkout, and order processing.
Incorporate essential integrations like payment gateways and potential shipping providers.

extracted.proposed_solution: Given the stringent 400-hour total project constraint, the proposed solution must significantly re-scope the initial comprehensive e-commerce platform. A Minimum Viable Product (MVP) approach is essential. The architecture will leverage a headless CMS and a Jamstack-like approach for the frontend to maximize development speed and frontend performance. Backend services will be optimized for core functionalities, possibly using serverless functions for specific tasks to reduce operational overhead and cost. Prioritization will focus on critical path features for a functional e-commerce experience: user authentication, product catalog display, basic shopping cart, a simplified checkout process (potentially with limited payment options initially), and essential order management for administrators. Advanced features like complex promotions, extensive analytics dashboards, and numerous integrations will be deferred to post-MVP phases. The technology stack will be streamlined, focusing on rapid development tools and managed cloud services (e.g., AWS Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB for simpler data models, or a highly optimized PostgreSQL). A smaller, highly efficient core team might be more appropriate than the initially suggested larger team, or the roles/responsibilities need to be condensed significantly to fit the time.

extracted.executive_summary: AuraCommerce Navigator aims to deliver a functional e-commerce website within a critical 400-hour total project constraint. This requires a significant re-scoping of the previously envisioned comprehensive platform. The focus will be on an MVP strategy, prioritizing core e-commerce functionalities: user authentication, product browsing, a streamlined shopping cart and checkout process, and basic order management. Advanced features and integrations will be considered for future iterations. The proposed solution leverages modern, efficient technologies such as Next.js for the frontend and potentially serverless architectures on AWS for the backend, ensuring rapid development and scalability. A lean, focused team and agile methodologies will be employed to meet the tight deadline. This approach balances the desire for a robust e-commerce presence with the practical limitations of the project timeline and resources.

raw_input: A full-stack web-based Hospital Management System (HMS) designed to streamline and digitize critical hospital operations including patient registration, doctor schedule coordination, real-time appointment booking, digital prescription management, and automated patient reminders. Developed with a Flask backend, a Vue.js frontend, Bootstrap responsive layouts, and SQLite/PostgreSQL databases, incorporating Celery and Redis to handle asynchronous background notification workflows.

extracted.normalized_text: A full-stack web-based Hospital Management System (HMS) designed to streamline and digitize critical hospital operations including patient registration, doctor schedule coordination, real-time appointment booking, digital prescription management, and automated patient reminders. Developed with a Flask backend, a Vue.js frontend, Bootstrap responsive layouts, and SQLite/PostgreSQL databases, incorporating Celery and Redis to handle asynchronous background notification workflows.
extracted.problem_statement: Traditional hospital and clinic workflows heavily depend on manual processes for maintaining medical histories, organizing doctor calendars, scheduling consultations, and executing patient follow-ups. These manual operations lead to high administration costs, structural scheduling conflicts, data transcription errors, and a high rate of missed appointments (no-shows) due to the absence of automated notification channels.
project_objectives: Design and deploy a centralized, secure web-based application to digitize patient, doctor, and administrative touchpoints.
Eliminate calendar overlaps and schedule conflicts through real-time reservation updates.
Implement an automated background notification subsystem to proactively alert patients of upcoming appointments, lowering clinic no-show rates.
Secure clinical and patient data using rigid Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) ensuring appropriate separation of duties.
extracted.proposed_solution: A modular, responsive Hospital Management System (HMS) consisting of a decoupled frontend built on Vue.js and a RESTful backend engineered in Flask. System data will be managed in a structured SQL database, backed by a Celery worker queue and Redis message broker. This system will support distinct interfaces for Patients, Doctors, and Administrators, with back-end automations managing time-sensitive notifications.
recommended_tools: Twilio API / SendGrid API (SMS and Email Delivery Gateway)
extracted.executive_summary: The CuraFlow HMS project addresses critical operational inefficiencies in healthcare facilities by replacing obsolete manual administrative tasks with a clean, roles-aware digital ecosystem. Built on lightweight yet robust technologies (Flask, Vue.js, Redis, and Celery), the platform ensures that administrative staff, medical professionals, and patients have tailored interfaces to perform administrative, clinical, and booking workflows. The crowning feature is an automated, queue-based reminder system designed to optimize clinic capacity and reduce costly missed consultations.

features.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
features.description: Restricts platform visibility and transactional capabilities depending on whether the authenticated user is a Patient, Doctor, or Administrator.
acceptance_criteria: Users can log in and are redirected to their designated dashboard.
Unauthenticated or unauthorized requests to secure endpoints return a 403 Forbidden status.
System administrators can modify user roles and permissions dynamically.

features.name: Self-Service Patient Portal
features.description: Allows patients to register, update personal demographic and basic medical history profiles, view historical consultations, and download active prescriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Patients can securely register using an email, phone number, and password.
Patients can view a comprehensive timeline of their historical appointments and diagnostic logs.
Prescriptions can be viewed and exported as a digital printout.

features.name: Doctor Calendar & Shift Management
features.description: Enables healthcare providers to define their working shifts, block off personal leaves, and specify standard consultation slot durations.
acceptance_criteria: Doctors can configure weekly recurring work schedules.
Calendar updates dynamically reflect available booking windows for patients.
The system prevents doctor schedule overlaps during reservation attempts.

features.name: Interactive Appointment Booking Engine
features.description: An interactive booking interface where patients can select clinical departments, assign a doctor, view real-time open slots, and submit appointment bookings.
acceptance_criteria: Booking lists update dynamically based on real-time doctor slot allocations.
A confirmation receipt is generated and emailed to the patient upon successful scheduling.
Double-booking of the same doctor-time slot is programmatically prevented.

features.name: Digital Prescriptions & Consultation Logs
features.description: Enables doctors to record consultation summaries, diagnostic observations, dosage parameters, and generate secure digital prescriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Doctors can write and save clinical notes and prescriptions directly to the patient's record.
Prescription updates are immediately visible in the respective Patient portal.
Records are timestamped and locked against unauthorized post-submission edits.

features.name: Asynchronous Appointment Reminder Engine
features.description: An automated background notification scheduler using Celery and Redis to monitor appointment timelines and dispatch SMS or email reminders to patients at set intervals.
acceptance_criteria: Automated alerts are queued and delivered exactly 24 hours prior to appointment times.
Failed notification attempts are logged and retried based on back-off rules.
Administrators can configure default template parameters for reminders.

features.name: Administrative Department & Staff Directory
features.description: An admin portal to manage medical departments, onboard doctors, configure platform settings, and monitor system metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Administrators can add, update, or deactivate departments and medical personnel.
Global configuration settings (e.g., appointment buffers, active reminder types) are manageable in-app.
An intuitive directory enables instant search and filtering of all staff records.

modules.name: Identity & Security Module
modules.summary: Handles OAuth/JWT authentication, role resolution, registration workflows, and session state management.

modules.name: Scheduling & Booking Core
modules.summary: Maintains calendar synchronization, doctor availability matrix, slot configuration, and reservation mechanics.

modules.name: Clinical Record Module
modules.summary: Facilitates prescription handling, charting, consultation histories, and diagnostic notes.

modules.name: Automation & Integration Gateway
modules.summary: Utilizes Celery workers and Redis message brokers to manage scheduled batch processing and event-driven notifications.

modules.name: Administrative Control Panel
modules.summary: The configuration nucleus for clinical departments, database backups, metadata catalogs, and staff profiles.

user_roles.name: Patient
responsibilities: Register account and keep personal medical histories up-to-date.
View active doctor availability schedules and book consultation slots.
Access active medical prescriptions and historical clinic consultation reports.

user_roles.name: Doctor
responsibilities: Manage working hours, break exceptions, and consultation durations.
Access chronological clinical queues of booked patient appointments.
Authorize and write diagnostic outcomes and patient pharmaceutical prescriptions.

user_roles.name: Administrator
responsibilities: Provision credentials, departments, and medical roles.
Oversee application metrics and check health statuses of message queues.
Modify notification parameters and resolve complex calendar resource conflicts.

data_models.name: User
data_models.description: The base identity record detailing login profiles, active status flags, and primary roles.

data_models.name: PatientProfile
data_models.description: Demographic details, emergency contacts, and generalized health logs mapped directly to a User.
attributes: user_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to User)

data_models.name: DoctorProfile
data_models.description: Professional taxonomy records mapping clinical specializations, departments, and consultation parameters.
attributes: user_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to User)
department_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to Department)

data_models.name: Appointment
data_models.description: The transaction log recording individual bookings, statuses, and links between patient and provider.
attributes: patient_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to PatientProfile)
doctor_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to DoctorProfile)

data_models.name: Prescription
data_models.description: A clinical direct-order entity generated post-consultation with medication steps.
attributes: appointment_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to Appointment)
medications (JSONB - listing compound names, dosages, intervals)

backend_jobs.name: Dynamic Appointment Reminder Dispatcher
backend_jobs.description: Checks for appointments scheduled precisely in the next 24 hours. Compiles messages and schedules delivery of SMS/Email notifications.

backend_jobs.name: Stale Reservation Auto-Cancel
backend_jobs.description: Identifies pending reservations that have not cleared validation milestones and auto-releases those slots back into open circulation.

constraints.description: The implementation must adhere to a full-stack architecture using a Flask-based RESTful API and a Vue.js single-page application (SPA).
Medical parameters and user details must align with localized health compliance acts (e.g., security equivalents of HIPAA/GDPR) regarding patient confidentiality.

non_functional_requirements.description: Core scheduling systems and booking interfaces must remain available to ensure continuous clinic access.
Frontend performance must optimize bundle footprints to deliver snappy view updates and prompt booking confirmations.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: API endpoints respond in under 300ms; frontend visual loading under 1.5 seconds under normal loads.

non_functional_requirements.description: Any change to patient diagnostic records, role classifications, or clinical logs must generate an unmodifiable audit trail.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: 100% of mutation events tracked in application log files with execution timestamps.

extracted.normalized_text: Enterprise Hospital Management and Electronic Health Records Platform
extracted.problem_statement: Modern healthcare organizations suffer from operational fragmentation, manual patient registration bottlenecks, prone-to-error paper scheduling, siloed medical records, and complex compliance reporting that threatens patient data security and overall clinic efficiency.
project_objectives: Provide an intuitive, responsive, and secure web interface for patients, medical staff, and hospital administrators.
Implement a robust Electronic Health Records (EHR) module that adheres to medical data compliance standards.
Automate clinical scheduling, appointment reminders, and personnel shifting.
Unify billing, copay collection, and insurance tracking into a centralized, transparent ledger.
Ensure total end-to-end auditability and absolute data protection to comply with global healthcare regulations.
extracted.proposed_solution: AegisHealth Portal is a secure, multi-tenant cloud platform built using microservices architecture. It integrates a patient portal for self-scheduling, clinical tools for medical staff (EHR, e-prescriptions, shift rosters), a robust financial module for medical billing and insurance, and automated auditing pipelines. High security is achieved through field-level database encryption, stringent RBAC, and strict adherence to HL7 FHIR data standards.
recommended_technologies: AWS RDS, AWS ECS, AWS KMS (Infrastructure and security management)
recommended_tools: Datadog & Sentry (System performance monitoring and error tracking)
GitHub Actions (CI/CD Pipeline with automated security scanning)
extracted.executive_summary: AegisHealth Portal replaces disparate legacy hospital workflows with a unified, cloud-native ERP. Designed for modern clinics and hospitals, it balances clinical performance with strict medical data privacy standards. The platform empowers patients to self-schedule, enables practitioners to manage patient charts efficiently, and gives administrators complete visibility over operational metrics and compliance records.

features.name: Patient Self-Registration & Onboarding
features.description: Secure sign-up portal enabling patients to upload demographic data, digital IDs, medical histories, insurance details, and sign digital consent forms.
acceptance_criteria: User passwords must be salted and hashed via bcrypt before database entry.
Patient profiles must support custom file uploads for insurance cards up to 5MB.
System must validate insurance policy formats using basic regex filters.

features.name: Dynamic Appointment Scheduling Engine
features.description: An interactive calendar interface allowing patients to book, reschedule, or cancel appointments based on real-time doctor availability with conflict resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Double-booking of the same doctor in overlapping time slots must be programmatically blocked.
Real-time update of doctor availability must reflect in under 1 second via WebSockets.
Patients must receive a confirmation email and SMS immediately upon booking.

features.name: Electronic Health Records (EHR) Manager
features.description: A highly secure portal for doctors and nurses to update diagnoses, document symptoms, write electronic prescriptions (e-RX), and view lab results.
acceptance_criteria: All updates to EHR data must be accompanied by an immutable audit trail entry.
Medical records must be encrypted at the column/field level inside PostgreSQL.
Doctors can export a patient's summary in a standardized PDF format.

features.name: Medical Billing, Invoicing & Insurance Integration
features.description: Generates itemized medical invoices automatically based on standard ICD-10 medical codes linked to consultations, enabling easy digital payment and claims filing.
acceptance_criteria: Billing items must map directly to updated ICD-10 medical coding tables.
Secure payments can be accepted directly via Stripe API.
The system must track payment statuses (Unpaid, Pending, Paid, Refunded).

features.name: Staff Shift & Roster Coordinator
features.description: Administrative workspace allowing head nurses and medical managers to build, publish, and edit shifts for medical practitioners, preventing staff burnout.
acceptance_criteria: Roster conflicts (e.g., overlapping shift assignments for a single nurse) must throw validation errors.
Medical staff can request shift swaps, triggering an approval flow for administrators.

features.name: Immutable Compliance & Access Auditor
features.description: A system dashboard for hospital compliance officers to inspect access logs, detailing exactly who accessed which Electronic Health Record, when, and from what IP address.
acceptance_criteria: Log entries cannot be modified or deleted by any system user, including administrators.
The system must generate alerts for anomalous behaviors (e.g., viewing 10+ medical charts in under 1 minute).

modules.name: Patient Management Module
modules.summary: Handles patient lifecycle events from self-registration, demographic management, dynamic scheduling, to telehealth queueing.

modules.name: Clinical Operations Module
modules.summary: Houses Electronic Health Records, practitioner schedules, prescription trackers, and clinical logs required for daily medical delivery.

modules.name: Financial & Administrative Module
modules.summary: Integrates clinical records with automated billing structures, secure payment gateways, and analytical dashboards.

modules.name: Compliance & Security Module
modules.summary: Provides core system configurations, granular RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) validations, and absolute, immutable system auditing logs.

user_roles.name: Patient
responsibilities: Manage personal contact information and upload insurance cards.
Book, reschedule, or cancel clinical appointments.
View historical lab summaries and pay outstanding bills online.

user_roles.name: Medical Practitioner (Doctor/Nurse)
responsibilities: Access authorized patient medical records during consultation.
Create diagnoses, authorize e-prescriptions, and order laboratory testing.
Track personal shift assignments and request schedule changes.

user_roles.name: Billing Administrator
responsibilities: Validate patient insurance parameters and confirm ICD-10 code accuracy.
Process copays, trigger payment reminders, and handle refunds.

user_roles.name: Compliance Officer / Super Admin
responsibilities: Manage platform-wide configurations, system integrations, and global roles.
Inspect immutable audit logs to verify strict regulatory compliance.
Override scheduling conflicts in emergency scenarios.

data_models.name: PatientProfile

data_models.name: Appointment
data_models.description: Defines an approved consultation window linking a specific patient, practitioner, and administrative state.

data_models.name: MedicalRecord

data_models.name: Invoice
data_models.description: Detailed transactional data representing costs incurred from hospital services.

backend_jobs.name: Appointment Reminder Trigger
backend_jobs.description: Queries upcoming scheduled appointments occurring within the next 24 hours and sends dynamic Email/SMS reminder messages to patients.

backend_jobs.name: Nightly Financial Reconciliation Job
backend_jobs.description: Calculates and packages all daily financial billing records, checking payment confirmation logs from Stripe, and generates summary invoices for the billing team.

backend_jobs.name: Audit Trail Archiving Routine
backend_jobs.description: Compresses and moves access records older than 90 days to cloud cold storage (e.g., AWS S3 Glacier Deep Archive) while maintaining hash integrity validations.

constraints.description: All system architectures and data schemas must be HIPAA compliant in the United States and GDPR compliant in the European Union regarding Personal Health Information (PHI).
EHR models must align to HL7 FHIR structures to easily permit integration with third-party laboratories.

non_functional_requirements.description: Data encryption must be present in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256) utilizing isolated key storage.

non_functional_requirements.description: Medical practitioners must experience low delays when retrieving patient records during active patient consults.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: Average API response time for EHR queries under 200 milliseconds

non_functional_requirements.description: The application must maintain exceptional uptime to guarantee access to emergency-related documentation.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: 99.99% system uptime across multi-region active-passive server distributions

raw_input: The project is a centralized web-based Hospital Management System (HMS) developed to replace manual healthcare workflows with automated, role-based digital tools. It streamlines patient registration, doctor scheduling, appointment booking, clinical prescription management, and background-based reminder automation.

extracted.normalized_text: The project is a centralized web-based Hospital Management System (HMS) developed to replace manual healthcare workflows with automated, role-based digital tools. It streamlines patient registration, doctor scheduling, appointment booking, clinical prescription management, and background-based reminder automation.
extracted.problem_statement: Traditional clinical and hospital workflows rely heavily on paper-based or disjointed manual processes. This leads to scheduling conflicts, difficulty in tracking patient medical history, slow communication between administrative staff, doctors, and patients, and high rates of missed appointments due to the lack of automated patient reminder systems.
project_objectives: To centralize administrative, scheduling, and medical record management in a single secure application.
To implement robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) ensuring appropriate separation of duties and medical data privacy for Admins, Doctors, and Patients.
To automate patient appointment reminder workflows using reliable background queues, reducing missed appointments.
To provide an intuitive interface for doctors to manage schedules and issue digital prescriptions during consultations.
extracted.proposed_solution: A responsive, modern full-stack Hospital Management System (HMS). The system utilizes an API-driven Python Flask backend, a modular Vue.js frontend styled with Bootstrap, and an SQL database layer. Complex workflows—such as asynchronous appointment reminder schedules—are orchestrated using Redis as a message broker and Celery as an asynchronous task worker.
recommended_technologies: PostgreSQL (Production-grade relational database instead of SQLite)
Bootstrap or Tailwind CSS (Responsive styling framework)
recommended_tools: Docker (Containerization of Flask, Celery, and Redis containers)
SendGrid / Twilio API (For email/SMS notification delivery)
Prometheus & Grafana (System performance and queue monitoring)
extracted.executive_summary: CuraFlow HMS is a web-based healthcare software system designed to digitize and automate critical hospital operations. Born out of an engineered proof-of-concept utilizing Flask, Vue.js, and Redis/Celery, this production-ready blueprint scales the design to meet high-availability cloud standards. It provides secure role-based portals for hospital administrators, clinical doctors, and patients, ensuring seamless registration, conflict-free scheduling, digital prescriptions, and automated reminders to optimize clinical operational efficiency.

features.name: Secure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
features.description: Provides user registration, login, and authorization. Separates clinical data access, scheduling permissions, and system configurations based on three specific roles: Patient, Doctor, and Administrator.
acceptance_criteria: Patients cannot view other patients' records or configure global system settings.
Doctors can view and write prescriptions only for patients under their care.
System administrators can manage all users, departments, and system parameters.

features.name: Dynamic Appointment Booking & Conflict Prevention
features.description: Enables patients to search for doctors by specialization or department, view available time slots, and schedule appointments. Built-in validation prevents double-booking of doctors.
acceptance_criteria: Prevent the booking of a doctor slot that overlaps with an existing appointment or off-duty hours.
Provide real-time booking status changes (Pending, Confirmed, Completed, Cancelled).
Enable patients to view, reschedule, or cancel their own bookings.

features.name: Clinical Prescription Management
features.description: Allows authorized doctors to create, edit, and append digital prescriptions during or after a patient consultation, storing the medical records securely.
acceptance_criteria: A prescription must include medication name, dosage details, frequency, and optional doctor notes.
Prescriptions must link uniquely to an appointment and a patient profile.
Patients can download their digital prescriptions as secure PDFs.

features.name: Asynchronous Notification & Reminder Engine
features.description: Utilizes a background worker framework to dynamically schedule and dispatch automated appointment reminders via email or SMS before the appointment time.
acceptance_criteria: Reminders must run asynchronously, ensuring that frontend user requests are never blocked.
The system must support configurable reminder intervals (e.g., exactly 24 hours prior to appointment).
Failure of a reminder dispatch must be automatically logged and retried up to three times.

features.name: Departmental and Staff Management
features.description: Enables system administrators to add new hospital departments, assign doctors to departments, and define operational hours.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can create, edit, or deactivate clinic departments.
Admin can assign doctor profiles to one or more active departments.
Changes in departments automatically update doctor filters on the patient appointment interface.

modules.name: Identity & Security Module
modules.summary: Handles OAuth/JWT based authentication, role verification, password hashing, and user profile setups.

modules.name: Scheduling Module
modules.summary: Coordinates calendar availabilities, appointment slot definitions, patient bookings, and real-time conflict-checking logic.

modules.name: Clinical Record Module
modules.summary: Enables data entry for patient medical history, vital signs, and generation of structured clinical prescriptions.

modules.name: Notification & Queue Module
modules.summary: Orchestrates background tasks, scheduling messages to Redis, and processing them via Celery to send push updates or emails.

modules.name: Administration Module
modules.summary: Administrative control center to configure clinic departments, manage staff directories, and review system audit logs.

user_roles.name: Administrator
responsibilities: Manage user accounts (activate/deactivate staff, doctors, and patient credentials).
Define hospital departments and monitor system-wide logs.

user_roles.name: Doctor
responsibilities: Manage active calendar schedules and daily shifts.
Review assigned patients' medical records and booking queues.
Create and publish digital prescriptions for patients.

user_roles.name: Patient
responsibilities: Search for medical staff and schedule appointments online.
View upcoming schedule, payment history, and download active prescriptions.

data_models.name: User
data_models.description: Represents a central authentication record for anyone logging into the CuraFlow system.

data_models.name: PatientProfile
data_models.description: Houses clinical and demographic details representing a patient.
attributes: user_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to User)

data_models.name: DoctorProfile
data_models.description: Represents specialized practitioners who can accept appointments and write prescriptions.
attributes: user_id (UUID - Foreign Key linking to User)

data_models.name: Appointment
data_models.description: Links a Patient, Doctor, and scheduled timeslot together.

data_models.name: Prescription
data_models.description: Clinical record generated during an appointment.
attributes: medications (JSON - array of drugs, dosages, frequencies)

backend_jobs.name: Send Appointment Reminders
backend_jobs.description: Queries the database for upcoming appointments scheduled exactly 24 hours in the future and enqueues notification tasks to Celery for email/SMS dispatch.

backend_jobs.name: Stale Appointment Auto-Expiry
backend_jobs.description: Scans for appointments that remain in a 'Pending' or 'Scheduled' status whose scheduled times have passed, transitioning them to 'Expired' status.

constraints.description: The system must support standard cloud container platforms, requiring fully parameterized Docker configurations for the backend, frontend, Redis, and workers.
All patient medical and demographic data must be treated as Protected Health Information (PHI). If deployed in commercial settings, database storage must support encryption-at-rest.

SQLite, while useful for lightweight academic environments, is prone to locking under high concurrency. Production environments require automated migration capabilities to modern databases like PostgreSQL.

non_functional_requirements.description: Secure access through JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and encryption of passwords using bcrypt.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: Zero clear-text passwords stored; 100% API endpoints authenticated (except public registration/login).

non_functional_requirements.description: Asynchronous tasks processed in background queues to prevent locking up application interface threads.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: HTTP API response times average < 200ms; background tasks process within 5 seconds of queue entry.

non_functional_requirements.description: Containerized high-availability configuration for critical application servers and message brokers.

constraints.description: UI Pages and Screen Design The following UI pages and screens are required for the Hospital Management System: Login Page (Authentication and Access Control): A secure login page for users to access the system.
Target: Full action history Availability: The platform should aim for high availability, with minimal downtime during operational hours.
Target: 99.5% uptime (excluding planned maintenance) Usability: The user interface should be intuitive and easy to navigate for both customers and administrators.
Maintainability: Codebase should be well-structured, documented, and employ consistent coding standards to facilitate future updates within the limited time.
Reliability & Uptime: Core scheduling systems and booking interfaces must remain available to ensure continuous clinic access.

Technologies and Design Constraints The Hospital Management System will utilize the following technologies and design constraints: Frontend: React.js for building reusable UI components Backend: Node.js with Express.js for creating RESTful APIs Database: PostgreSQL for relational data storage Authentication: OAuth 2.0 for secure authentication Deployment: AWS Elastic Beanstalk for cloud-based deployment Containerization: Docker for containerization API Gateway: NGINX for API gateway and load balancing SPECIFIC USER FEEDBACK TO ADDRESS (Attempt #1): no need of any otrher moduel except patient, doctor and admin and dashboard has to be separte dashboard and appointment mangement module CRITICAL: Preserve all previously requested changes unless the new feedback explicitly replaces them.

features.name: User Login & MFA
features.description: Secure user login with multi-factor authentication (MFA) and token-based session management.
acceptance_criteria: User can authenticate using password and an MFA OTP token.
System generates valid JWT sessions.
Failed attempts lock accounts after 5 consecutive failures.

features.name: User Registration & Password Recovery
features.description: Seamless self-service account registration with email verification and self-service password recovery workflows.
acceptance_criteria: User can register via a public signup page.
Activation link is sent to verified email.
Self-service password recovery with secure token verification.

features.name: Interactive Landing Dashboard
features.description: A personalized homepage showcasing key metrics, system status, interactive activity graphs, and quick actions.
acceptance_criteria: Users see a customized landing page based on their specific role.
Displays real-time system performance statistics and active counts.
Includes responsive grid layout with interactive data visualization widgets.

features.name: Custom Report Generator
features.description: Advanced query interface allowing administrators to generate, customize, and export operational reports.
acceptance_criteria: Generates reports filtering by date range, module, and user activity.
Supports exporting data in PDF and CSV format.
Saves customized report queries for future access.

features.name: User Profile Management
features.description: Allows authenticated users to view, edit, and update their personal profiles, avatars, and security preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Users can update contact info, upload avatar, and change password.
Enforces strong password complexity check during update.
Sends confirmation email upon key profile modifications.

features.name: Organization Configurations
features.description: System-wide settings for enterprise rules, notification settings, white-label configurations, and API integrations.
acceptance_criteria: System administrators can modify system name, theme, and logo.
Toggle toggleable system-wide features.
Configure SMTP email server settings and test connections.

features.name: Compliance Audit Logger
features.description: Automated background logging of all database write operations, authentication attempts, and authorization changes.
acceptance_criteria: Unalterable recording of user, IP, timestamp, action, and diff payload.
Logs are stored in an append-only archive.
Captures unauthorized access attempts immediately as critical warning flags.

features.name: Activity Log Viewer
features.description: Interactive interface for audit team members to search, filter, and inspect detailed audit trails.
acceptance_criteria: Search records by username, date range, or action type.
Export filtered query results directly into an Excel/PDF archive.
Display color-coded severity indicators for fast review.

extracted.executive_summary: The Hospital Management System (HMS) will provide a role-based access control platform for patients, doctors, and admins to manage hospital operations. The system will be built using a microservices architecture and will be deployed on AWS Cloud.

features.name: Role-Based Access Control
features.description: The system will have role-based access control for patients, doctors, and admins
acceptance_criteria: The system will authenticate and authorize users based on their roles
The system will restrict access to sensitive data based on user roles

features.name: Patient Management
features.description: The system will allow patients to view their medical records, schedule appointments, and communicate with doctors
acceptance_criteria: The system will display patient medical records
The system will allow patients to schedule appointments
The system will enable communication between patients and doctors

features.name: Doctor Management
features.description: The system will allow doctors to view patient records, schedule appointments, and communicate with patients
acceptance_criteria: The system will display patient records
The system will allow doctors to schedule appointments
The system will enable communication between doctors and patients

features.name: Admin Management
features.description: The system will allow admins to manage hospital operations, including user management, system configuration, and reporting
acceptance_criteria: The system will allow admins to manage users
The system will allow admins to configure system settings
The system will provide reporting features

features.name: User Login & MFA
features.description: Secure user login with multi-factor authentication (MFA) and token-based session management.
acceptance_criteria: User can authenticate using password and an MFA OTP token.
System generates valid JWT sessions.
Failed attempts lock accounts after 5 consecutive failures.

features.name: User Registration & Password Recovery
features.description: Seamless self-service account registration with email verification and self-service password recovery workflows.
acceptance_criteria: User can register via a public signup page.
Activation link is sent to verified email.
Self-service password recovery with secure token verification.

features.name: Interactive Landing Dashboard
features.description: A personalized homepage showcasing key metrics, system status, interactive activity graphs, and quick actions.
acceptance_criteria: Users see a customized landing page based on their specific role.
Displays real-time system performance statistics and active counts.
Includes responsive grid layout with interactive data visualization widgets.

features.name: Custom Report Generator
features.description: Advanced query interface allowing administrators to generate, customize, and export operational reports.
acceptance_criteria: Generates reports filtering by date range, module, and user activity.
Supports exporting data in PDF and CSV format.
Saves customized report queries for future access.

features.name: User Profile Management
features.description: Allows authenticated users to view, edit, and update their personal profiles, avatars, and security preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Users can update contact info, upload avatar, and change password.
Enforces strong password complexity check during update.
Sends confirmation email upon key profile modifications.

features.name: Organization Configurations
features.description: System-wide settings for enterprise rules, notification settings, white-label configurations, and API integrations.
acceptance_criteria: System administrators can modify system name, theme, and logo.
Toggle toggleable system-wide features.
Configure SMTP email server settings and test connections.

features.name: Compliance Audit Logger
features.description: Automated background logging of all database write operations, authentication attempts, and authorization changes.
acceptance_criteria: Unalterable recording of user, IP, timestamp, action, and diff payload.
Logs are stored in an append-only archive.
Captures unauthorized access attempts immediately as critical warning flags.

features.name: Activity Log Viewer
features.description: Interactive interface for audit team members to search, filter, and inspect detailed audit trails.
acceptance_criteria: Search records by username, date range, or action type.
Export filtered query results directly into an Excel/PDF archive.
Display color-coded severity indicators for fast review.

modules.name: Authentication and Access Control
modules.summary: The module will handle user authentication and role-based access control

modules.name: Patient Management
modules.summary: The module will handle patient-related features

modules.name: Doctor Management

modules.summary: The module will handle admin-related features

modules.name: Authentication & Access Control
modules.summary: Handles secure user authentication, multi-factor authorization, self-service registration, and role-based access control policies across all interfaces.

modules.name: Dashboard & Reporting
modules.summary: Aggregates real-time activities and compiles customized performance reports, analytics charts, and visual dashboards.

modules.name: System Settings & Profiles
modules.summary: Allows users to personalize settings and administrators to adjust global parameters, localization, and system operations.

modules.name: Audit Trail & Activity Logs
modules.summary: Tracks all actions within the system, recording changes to security rules, database records, and configuration logs for audit compliance.

backend_jobs.name: Audit Trail Archiving Routine
backend_jobs.description: Compresses and moves access records older than 90 days to cloud cold storage (e.g., AWS S3 Glacier Deep Archive) while maintaining hash integrity validations.

constraints.description: Organization Configurations: System-wide settings for enterprise rules, notification settings, white-label configurations, and API integrations.
External Interface Requirements The system must provide a user-friendly interface for patients, doctors, and admins to interact with the system.
The system must securely process and store sensitive patient data.
The system must authenticate users and authorize access to certain features based on user roles.
The system must provide administrators with tools to manage user accounts, system settings, and reporting.
The system must track key user actions and maintain a history log for audit purposes.

non_functional_requirements.description: Ensure application security and compliance
Optimize application performance
Keep detailed audit trails
The system must be able to handle a growing customer base.
The user interface should be intuitive and easy to navigate for both customers and administrators.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: User testing confirms task completion rate > 90% for core journeys.

non_functional_requirements.description: Core scheduling systems and booking interfaces must remain available to ensure continuous clinic access.

ui_pages.description: The patient profile page for patients to view their medical records and appointment history

ui_pages.name: Doctor Profile
ui_pages.description: The doctor profile page for doctors to view their patient records and appointment history

ui_pages.name: Admin Settings
ui_pages.description: The admin settings page for admins to manage system settings and user accounts

ui_pages.description: User self-registration form and secure password reset workflow interface.

ui_pages.description: Dynamic querying workspace to construct, preview, and download custom logs or summaries.

ui_pages.description: Personal account information form with active session tracking list.

ui_pages.description: System configuration screen for setting administrative thresholds and workspace preferences.

ui_pages.description: Searchable table log detailing system actions, active logins, and detailed state revisions.

raw_input: build a webiste for hospital mangement system it has 3 user roles admin, doctor and patient

extracted.normalized_text: An enterprise-grade, web-based Hospital Management System (HMS) and Electronic Health Record (EHR) platform featuring multi-tenant role-based access control for Administrators, Medical Practitioners (Doctors), and Patients.
extracted.problem_statement: Modern healthcare facilities struggle with fragmented workflows, insecure communication channels, and administrative bottlenecks. Paper-based or legacy EHR systems lead to scheduling conflicts, billing errors, delayed clinical decision-making, and high risks of HIPAA compliance violations. Hospitals require a secure, unified, and real-time digital workspace that connects patients, medical professionals, and administrators seamlessly.
project_objectives: To centralize administrative, clinical, and patient activities into a secure, HIPAA-compliant web portal.
To automate scheduling and eliminate double-booking of doctors and clinical resources.
To digitize patient health records (EHR/EMR) ensuring secure, immediate access for authorized clinical staff.
To streamline billing, payment processing, and insurance claims tracking.
To empower patients with self-service booking, history access, and secure messaging portals.
extracted.proposed_solution: HealSync EHR is an integrated SaaS-ready Hospital Management System built on a secure microservices architecture. It features a Next.js responsive frontend, a high-performance FastAPI/Go backend, and a dual-database layer (PostgreSQL for transactional billing/scheduling; MongoDB for semi-structured clinical EHR data). The system implements rigorous HIPAA-compliant encryption (AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit) and fine-grained Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). It includes specialized modules for automated scheduling, real-time Telemedicine via WebRTC, structured E-Prescriptions with interaction warnings, and an automated medical billing system.
recommended_technologies: Next.js (React 18+, TypeScript, TailwindCSS) for high-performance frontend
FastAPI (Python) for robust, high-performance, and secure clinical APIs
PostgreSQL for ACID-compliant structured relational data (Users, Billing, Schedules)
MongoDB for flexible schema Electronic Health Records (EHR) document storage
Redis for real-time session caching and lock management during slot bookings
AWS KMS & AWS Shield for HIPAA-compliant encryption and security safeguarding
WebRTC & Daily.co SDK for native telehealth video consultations
recommended_tools: Docker & Kubernetes for containerized microservice deployments
GitHub Actions for automated CI/CD pipelines with integrated security scanning
Sentry for real-time error reporting and application monitoring
Datadog for infrastructure logging and APM compliance auditing
Postman for end-to-end API contract testing
extracted.executive_summary: HealSync EHR is a high-availability, HIPAA-compliant hospital management web application designed to bridge the gap between patients, doctors, and clinical administrators. By automating scheduling, clinical charting, prescription management, and billing, the platform reduces operational overhead by up to 40% and significantly improves patient clinical outcomes. Built using secure, modern cloud architectures, HealSync EHR ensures maximum data privacy, system resilience, and compliance with global healthcare data standards.

features.name: Multi-Factor Authentication & RBAC Engine
features.description: Secure, HIPAA-compliant login utilizing OAuth2/OIDC, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), and strict Role-Based Access Control targeting Administrators, Doctors, Patients, and general Staff.
acceptance_criteria: Users can log in using email, password, and a TOTP second factor (Google Authenticator).
Sessions automatically timeout after 15 minutes of idle time to prevent unauthorized access.
Attempting to access unauthorized routes returns a 403 Forbidden response.

features.name: Dynamic Clinical Dashboards
features.description: Role-specific real-time landing dashboards presenting quick-stats. Admins view bed occupancy and daily revenue; Doctors view daily appointment queues and patient vitals; Patients view upcoming appointments and prescription alerts.
acceptance_criteria: Doctors dashboard loads upcoming 5 appointments within 200ms.
Admin dashboard aggregates financial metrics with a visual line chart.
Patient dashboard clearly displays pending action items (e.g., unpaid invoices, incomplete intakes).

features.name: Interactive Appointment Scheduler
features.description: A real-time calendar and scheduling engine enabling patients to book slots based on doctor-defined availability, with automatic double-booking prevention and time-zone conversions.
acceptance_criteria: System locks a selected time-slot for 5 minutes during checkout to prevent double-booking.
Doctors can define recurring weekly shift schedules and blackout dates.
Patients receive automated confirmation emails and calendar invites (.ics file) upon successful booking.

features.name: Electronic Health Record (EHR) Module
features.description: A secure digital vault for storing structured patient clinical histories, lab results, diagnoses, and vitals, fully editable by doctors and readable by patients.
acceptance_criteria: Doctors can create, read, update, and sign-off clinical soap notes.
Any update to the EHR records creates an immutable log containing user, timestamp, and changes.
Medical records must support secure file uploads (PDFs, DICOM medical images) with malware screening.

features.name: E-Prescription (eRx) Engine
features.description: Enables doctors to issue electronic prescriptions, including dosage instructions, refills, and automated warnings for potential drug-allergy or drug-drug interactions.
acceptance_criteria: Doctors can search an FDA-approved drug database to compile a prescription.
System alerts the doctor if a selected drug matches a patient's documented allergies.
Prescriptions generate a unique QR code and signed PDF download link for pharmacies.

features.name: Automated Medical Billing & Invoicing
features.description: Generates itemized invoices automatically based on consultation fees, laboratory tests, and prescribed drugs, with integrations for online payment gateways and insurance processing.
acceptance_criteria: Generates an itemized invoice instantly when a doctor closes a clinical encounter.
Integrates with Stripe/PayPal to accept credit card, Apple Pay, and ACH payments.
Allows administrators to record insurance policy details and track pre-authorization status.

features.name: Immutable Security Audit Trail
features.description: An administrative ledger tracking every read, write, update, and deletion of Protected Health Information (PHI) to guarantee HIPAA and SOC2 compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Any access of a patient's medical file generates a non-deletable log entry.
Logs must include Actor ID, Action Type, Timestamp, Target Patient ID, and Client IP.
Administrators can query and filter logs by date range and Doctor ID.

features.name: Telehealth Video Consultations
features.description: An integrated peer-to-peer and SFU video conferencing room embedded directly into the doctor and patient dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: Launches video calls directly inside the web interface without external application requirements.
Provides end-to-end encrypted screen-sharing and real-time chat during calls.
Enables doctors to write clinical notes in a side panel while keeping the video active.

features.name: User Login & MFA
features.description: Secure user login with multi-factor authentication (MFA) and token-based session management.
acceptance_criteria: User can authenticate using password and an MFA OTP token.
System generates valid JWT sessions.
Failed attempts lock accounts after 5 consecutive failures.

features.name: User Registration & Password Recovery
features.description: Seamless self-service account registration with email verification and self-service password recovery workflows.
acceptance_criteria: User can register via a public signup page.
Activation link is sent to verified email.
Self-service password recovery with secure token verification.

features.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
features.description: Comprehensive definition of user roles and permission matrices to secure API endpoints and UI pathways.
acceptance_criteria: Administrator can assign roles to specific user accounts.
Restricted routes return 403 Forbidden for unauthorized roles.
Permissions are enforced at the API level.

features.name: Interactive Landing Dashboard
features.description: A personalized homepage showcasing key metrics, system status, interactive activity graphs, and quick actions.
acceptance_criteria: Users see a customized landing page based on their specific role.
Displays real-time system performance statistics and active counts.
Includes responsive grid layout with interactive data visualization widgets.

features.name: Custom Report Generator
features.description: Advanced query interface allowing administrators to generate, customize, and export operational reports.
acceptance_criteria: Generates reports filtering by date range, module, and user activity.
Supports exporting data in PDF and CSV format.
Saves customized report queries for future access.

features.name: User Profile Management
features.description: Allows authenticated users to view, edit, and update their personal profiles, avatars, and security preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Users can update contact info, upload avatar, and change password.
Enforces strong password complexity check during update.
Sends confirmation email upon key profile modifications.

features.name: Organization Configurations
features.description: System-wide settings for enterprise rules, notification settings, white-label configurations, and API integrations.
acceptance_criteria: System administrators can modify system name, theme, and logo.
Toggle toggleable system-wide features.
Configure SMTP email server settings and test connections.

features.name: Compliance Audit Logger
features.description: Automated background logging of all database write operations, authentication attempts, and authorization changes.
acceptance_criteria: Unalterable recording of user, IP, timestamp, action, and diff payload.
Logs are stored in an append-only archive.
Captures unauthorized access attempts immediately as critical warning flags.

features.name: Activity Log Viewer
features.description: Interactive interface for audit team members to search, filter, and inspect detailed audit trails.
acceptance_criteria: Search records by username, date range, or action type.
Export filtered query results directly into an Excel/PDF archive.
Display color-coded severity indicators for fast review.

features.name: User Login & Session Security
features.description: Secure login with protected sessions, failed-attempt handling, and logout controls.
acceptance_criteria: Users can log in and log out securely.
Sessions expire after inactivity.
Failed login attempts are rate-limited or locked after repeated failures.

features.name: Role-Based Dashboard
features.description: Landing dashboard with metrics, quick actions, and status widgets tailored by user role.
acceptance_criteria: Users see dashboard content relevant to their role.
Key operational metrics are visible after login.
Dashboard widgets link to primary workflows.

features.name: Report Generation & Export
features.description: Generate filtered reports and export operational data for business review.
acceptance_criteria: Users can filter report data by core business dimensions.
Reports can be exported in common formats.
Generated reports respect role permissions.

features.name: Application Configuration
features.description: Administrative configuration for organization settings, defaults, and system preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Administrators can update global settings.
System configuration changes are validated.
Critical configuration changes are audit logged.

features.name: Audit Event Logging
features.description: Capture authentication events, authorization failures, data changes, and admin actions.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitive actions create audit records.
Audit records include user, timestamp, action, and target entity.
Audit logs cannot be edited by regular users.

modules.name: Authentication & Access Control
modules.summary: Handles OAuth2/MFA authentication, session management, and role-based permissions across patients, doctors, and admins.

modules.name: Dashboard & Reporting Engine
modules.summary: Generates dynamic real-time reporting dashboards tailored to system, clinical, or personal health metrics.

modules.name: Appointment & Telemedicine Module
modules.summary: Handles doctor scheduling schedules, booking configurations, real-time calendars, and HIPAA-secure video consultations.

modules.name: Clinical EHR & Prescriptions
modules.summary: Core Electronic Health Records domain managing SOAP clinical charts, patient medical history, uploads, and automated interaction-warned prescription generation.

modules.name: Financials & Billing Operations
modules.summary: Tracks encounter costs, generates compliant medical invoices, manages payment processing, and integrates with insurance policies.

modules.name: Audit & Compliance Logging
modules.summary: Enforces HIPAA and regulatory compliance through system-wide monitoring, immutability ledgers, and access tracing.

modules.name: System Settings & Profiles
modules.summary: Allows users to personalize settings and administrators to adjust global parameters, localization, and system operations.

user_roles.name: Administrator
responsibilities: Onboard and verify clinical licenses of doctors and medical staff.
Configure hospital structures, department schedules, and billing rates.
Monitor compliance metrics and execute security audit reports.
Manage overall system-level infrastructure and configuration.

user_roles.name: Doctor
responsibilities: Define weekly shift availability and manage appointment schedules.
Access, update, and manage patient Electronic Health Records (EHR).
Record symptoms, update diagnoses, and write clinical encounter notes.
Conduct video telemedicine consults with patients.

user_roles.name: Patient
responsibilities: Search and view profiles of clinical specialists and doctors.
Schedule, reschedule, or cancel consultation appointments online.
Access personal health histories, laboratory results, and active prescriptions.
Securely message doctors or make invoice payments online.

data_models.name: User
data_models.description: Represents a system user identity with specific role and profile linkages.

data_models.name: PatientProfile
data_models.description: Stores the demographic and medical registration metrics of a patient.

data_models.name: DoctorProfile
data_models.description: Details clinical expertise and verification settings for medical staff.

data_models.name: Appointment
data_models.description: Represents a scheduled booking between a patient and a doctor.
attributes: status: Enum (PENDING, CONFIRMED, CANCELLED, COMPLETED, NO_SHOW)

data_models.name: EHRRecord
data_models.description: Stores clinical encounter summaries, histories, and diagnoses (MongoDB optimization).

data_models.name: Prescription
data_models.description: Maintains details of medications issued to patients.

data_models.name: AuditLog
data_models.description: Immutable ledger tracking clinical security and system interactions.

backend_jobs.name: Automated Appointment Reminders
backend_jobs.description: Queries the database for upcoming appointments scheduled in exactly 24 hours. Sends automated SMS and email reminders to patients containing check-in instructions.

backend_jobs.name: Temporary Booking Lock Cleaner
backend_jobs.description: Scans Redis cache to identify and delete expired appointment slot locks (held longer than 5 minutes) to ensure slots are released back to public inventory.

backend_jobs.name: Audit Log Archival Job
backend_jobs.description: Compresses audit logs older than 90 days, hashes them for verification, and archives them to cold, secure cloud storage (e.g., AWS Glacier) for multi-year retention compliance.

constraints.description: The application must strictly adhere to the HIPAA Security Rule. All Protected Health Information (PHI) must be encrypted at-rest using AES-256 and in-transit using TLS 1.3.
All clinical, personal, and patient records must be stored strictly within databases located inside the native regulatory geographic boundary (e.g., US regions for HIPAA, EU regions for GDPR).

non_functional_requirements.description: Implement continuous audit log generation and automated penetration scans.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: Zero critical or high CVE vulnerabilities detected on third-party libraries; 100% compliance with HIPAA audit guidelines.

non_functional_requirements.description: System architecture must minimize single points of failure to guarantee hospital uptime availability.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: 99.95% system uptime over any calendar month, translating to less than 22 minutes of unscheduled downtime.

non_functional_requirements.description: API response latency must be optimized to ensure physicians can efficiently navigate records during consultations.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: 95% of read-only clinical dashboard queries must return in under 250 milliseconds under peak concurrent load of 1,000 active users.

non_functional_requirements.description: The system must record security-sensitive actions, administrative changes, and important business events.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: Critical user and admin actions are logged with actor, timestamp, action, and target record.

planning_assumptions: Team model: 12 total members — 8 developers, 4 testers.
Total raw effort: 218 dev days + 80 test days.
Estimated calendar duration with recommended team: ~9 weeks.
Module boundaries and timings should be reviewed before committing to delivery contracts.
7 modules identified across 22 features.

assumptions: Patients are responsible for keeping their login credentials secure and enabling MFA on their personal devices.
Integrated medical billing assumes external payment gateways (like Stripe or medical clearinghouses) will handle actual bank clearing and direct wire transfers.
A reliable internet connection is available to both doctors and patients for the real-time video streaming (Telehealth) feature to function properly.
ai_observations: Because medical software deals with Protected Health Information (PHI), standard databases should utilize Field-Level Encryption (FLE) for attributes like SSNs, dates of birth, and clinical diagnosis codes.

Integrating standard HL7 FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) data models would be highly beneficial in the future to allow this web system to interact directly with external legacy laboratory systems and pharmacy vendors.

The Telehealth feature will require TURN/STUN servers (e.g., coturn) configured in the infrastructure to handle video streaming bypass when patients are behind restrictive corporate or residential NATs.
Baseline application scope was enforced: authentication, access control, dashboard/reporting, settings, audit logging, security, and deployment-ready traceability.
extracted.conclusion: HealSync EHR is an optimally balanced, highly secure, and feature-rich hospital management platform. By implementing role-specific workflow models for Admins, Doctors, and Patients, and structuring modular architectures for scheduling, EHRs, billing, and telehealth, this system solves the core coordination and security compliance problems modern healthcare facilities face.

extracted.normalized_text: An enterprise-grade, HIPAA-compliant Hospital Management System (HMS) featuring a web-based portal designed for three principal user roles: Administrators, Doctors, and Patients. The system provides secure Authentication, Patient Medical Records, Clinical Management, Appointment Scheduling, Resource Allocation, and comprehensive Audit Logs.
extracted.problem_statement: Modern healthcare facilities face critical inefficiencies due to fragmented patient data, high administrative overhead in appointment booking, lack of clear visibility into clinical schedules, and non-compliance with rigorous healthcare security standards like HIPAA. Without a centralized digital platform, scheduling conflicts, patient delays, and coordination bottlenecks frequently compromise patient care quality.
project_objectives: Centralize administrative, clinical, and patient engagement workflows into a unified, secure web platform.
Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) to restrict health record access based on verified credentials.
Streamline doctor-patient engagement through online scheduling, digital prescription generation, and automated reminders.
Establish comprehensive, tamper-proof audit trails for administrative activities and clinical data alterations to ensure standard healthcare compliance.
extracted.proposed_solution: AegisHealth Nexus is a cloud-native, high-performance web portal built on a modern decoupled architecture. It utilizes a secure Node.js micro-framework backend powered by PostgreSQL for high-integrity relational clinical records, with a React/Next.js frontend delivering real-time responsive updates. The system provides role-optimized workspaces for Admins to manage resources, Doctors to manage clinical treatments, and Patients to self-schedule, view prescriptions, and handle billings securely.
recommended_technologies: AWS S3 (Secure document storage for medical scans)
extracted.executive_summary: AegisHealth Nexus bridges the digital gap in clinical operations by providing a fast, secure, and intuitive web application tailored for patient care. Combining administrative resource management, precise scheduling algorithms, secure digital prescriptions, and standard-compliant audit logs, AegisHealth Nexus drives clinical throughput, eliminates administrative overhead, and guarantees secure custody of sensitive healthcare data.

features.name: Secure Multi-Role Authentication
features.description: Enterprise-level Identity and Access Management featuring secure multi-factor authentication (MFA), secure JWT session management, account lockout mechanisms, and strict Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) rules mapped to system modules.
acceptance_criteria: System must enforce passwords to have minimum 12 characters, uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.
Sessions must auto-expire after 15 minutes of inactivity.
Attempting to access unauthorized routes must return a 403 Forbidden status code.

features.name: Self-Service Patient Registration and Profiles
features.description: Allows patients to register online, undergo digital identity verification, and build their comprehensive personal profile including emergency contacts, chronic conditions, and insurance policies.
acceptance_criteria: Patient must upload a valid ID document and complete demographic data.
System must validate insurance policy numbers using mock validation rules.
Profiles must be fully editable by the owner, with updates logged in audit logs.

features.name: Interactive Appointment Scheduler
features.description: An interactive calendar interface allowing patients to view doctor availability slots in real time, book appointments, reschedule existing slots, and request cancellations based on clinical availability rules.
acceptance_criteria: System must prevent double-booking of any doctor's time slot.
Patients must be limited to a maximum of 3 pending appointments at a time.
Doctor's dashboard calendar must update via WebSockets within 2 seconds of a booking.

features.name: Clinical EHR and Progress Notes
features.description: A secure digital workflow for doctors to input SOAP (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan) clinical notes, log patient vital signs, update active medical diagnoses, and upload radiological/laboratory imaging.
acceptance_criteria: Only assigned doctors can write or edit a patient's active health records.
Changes to existing progress notes must create a historical version, preventing overwrites of historical medical facts.
All updates must trigger an audit trail entry listing clinical editor's user ID and timestamp.

features.name: Electronic Prescription Module
features.description: Digital prescription interface allowing doctors to select approved medications, adjust dosages, specify frequencies, and authorize renewals, direct-dispatching records to patient portals.
acceptance_criteria: Prescription creation form must enforce validation of standard drug lists.
Prescriptions must contain digital signatures of the writing clinician.
Patient portal must display active prescriptions instantly with a 'Print to PDF' capability.

features.name: Clinical Staff Allocation and Resource Panel
features.description: A centralized control panel for Administrators to organize clinician shift patterns, manage ward/bed allocations, and oversee general operational availability.
acceptance_criteria: Admins can override doctor shift templates to configure custom emergency or vacation windows.
System must flag scheduling conflicts where doctor assignments conflict with off-duty schedules.
Changes must automatically broadcast to doctor schedules instantly.

features.name: System Audit Trail & Compliance Logging
features.description: A robust, cryptographically chain-linked transaction audit system tracking every data-read, modification, or session state change involving protected health information (PHI) to conform with HIPAA regulations.
acceptance_criteria: Logs must contain exact timestamps, user identities, client IP addresses, action types, and old/new database states.
Logs must be read-only and immutable, even to Administrator accounts.
Administrators must have dedicated interfaces to query audits with filtering by user or module.

features.name: Billing & Medical Invoicing Engine

acceptance_criteria: Invoice status must dynamically switch between 'Unpaid', 'Pending Insurance Processing', and 'Paid'.
System must automatically compute tax and total fees based on clinic pricing rules.
Patients must have visual notification on login of unpaid medical invoices.

acceptance_criteria: Data metrics must reload at least once every 10 minutes.
Data exports must support XLS and PDF formats.
Visual charts must render correctly on both desktop and tablet screen sizes.

Reports can be exported in common formats.
Generated reports respect role permissions.

features.name: Application Configuration
features.description: Administrative configuration for organization settings, defaults, and system preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Administrators can update global settings.
System configuration changes are validated.
Critical configuration changes are audit logged.

features.name: Audit Event Logging
features.description: Capture authentication events, authorization failures, data changes, and admin actions.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitive actions create audit records.
Audit records include user, timestamp, action, and target entity.

modules.name: Authentication & Access Control
modules.summary: Handles patient, doctor, and administrator identity verifications, session lifecycles, and enforces fine-grained RBAC rule evaluations across system endpoints.

modules.name: Patient Management Portal
modules.summary: Integrates self-service onboarding, medical demographic registries, appointments booking calendars, personal medication views, and active invoicing.

modules.name: Clinical Operations Suite
modules.summary: Equips clinicians with diagnostic interfaces, secure SOAP notes writing tools, vital tracking timelines, and integrated electronic prescriptions workflow.

modules.name: Hospital Administration & Resource Engine
modules.summary: Empowers operational administrators to control workforce schedules, allocate clinic facilities, track dynamic operational metrics, and review critical compliance paths.
feature_names: Clinical Staff Allocation and Resource Panel

modules.name: Audit & Security Infrastructure
modules.summary: Continuous operational monitoring engine capturing transactional actions, clinical modifications, and user state variations for security forensics and HIPAA audit compliance.

modules.name: Dashboard & Reporting
modules.summary: Aggregates real-time activities and compiles customized performance reports, analytics charts, and visual dashboards.

modules.name: System Settings & Profiles
modules.summary: Allows users to personalize settings and administrators to adjust global parameters, localization, and system operations.

user_roles.name: Administrator
responsibilities: Manage clinical staff profiles, shift rotations, and scheduling availability guidelines.
Monitor operational efficiency metrics, capacity utilization, and financial revenue flows.
Access global system configurations and query the compliance Audit Log for security audits.

user_roles.name: Doctor
responsibilities: Access assigned patient profiles and historic clinical medical records.
Update diagnoses, manage vital charts, log medical assessments, and input digital progress notes.
Generate and digitally sign patient medical prescriptions and therapeutic plans.

user_roles.name: Patient
responsibilities: Manage personal profile records, insurance data, and physical addresses.
Schedule, reschedule, or cancel clinical medical appointments through self-service calendars.
Review assigned digital prescriptions and settle outstanding clinical service invoices.

data_models.name: User
data_models.description: Represents basic identity profile information, authentication keys, and assigned security role authorization tags.

data_models.name: PatientProfile
data_models.description: Contains demographics, emergency data, and clinical identifiers of physical patients linked back to their system login identity.
attributes: user_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to User)

data_models.name: DoctorProfile
data_models.description: Stores clinical operational profiles of doctors, active credentials, specialization areas, and shift rosters.
attributes: user_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to User)

data_models.name: Appointment
data_models.description: Represents instances of planned physical or virtual patient consultations and clinic resource blocks.
attributes: patient_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to PatientProfile)
doctor_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to DoctorProfile)

data_models.name: ElectronicHealthRecord
data_models.description: Transactional core tracking medical summaries, vital telemetry, diagnoses, and medical findings per individual consultation.
attributes: patient_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to PatientProfile)
doctor_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to DoctorProfile)

data_models.name: Prescription
data_models.description: Records list of authorized medications, timing parameters, and electronic signatures generated by clinicians.
attributes: appointment_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to Appointment)
doctor_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to DoctorProfile)
patient_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to PatientProfile)
medication_data: JSONB (drug name, dose, frequency, duration)

data_models.name: Invoice
data_models.description: Manages billings, insurance adjustments, and transaction tallies linked to clinic patient services.
attributes: patient_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to PatientProfile)
appointment_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to Appointment)

data_models.name: AuditLog
data_models.description: Read-only ledger tracking actions taken, client details, and before-after database states.
attributes: actor_user_id: UUID (Foreign Key linking to User)

backend_jobs.name: AppointmentReminderDispatcher
backend_jobs.description: Queries upcoming appointments scheduled within the next 24 hours and dispatches secure push notifications or transactional emails to both patients and doctors.

backend_jobs.name: DailyScheduleOverdueUpdater
backend_jobs.description: Finds all appointments in 'PENDING' or 'CONFIRMED' statuses from previous calendar days that were never marked as 'COMPLETED' or 'CANCELLED' and updates them to 'MISSED'.

backend_jobs.name: BillingOverdueNotifier
backend_jobs.description: Triggers billing system alerts, automatically updating unpaid invoices past due dates to 'OVERDUE' and emailing reminders to patients with a quick payment invoice portal link.

constraints.description: All processes accessing, presenting, or storing PHI (Protected Health Information) must satisfy HIPAA and HITECH requirements, including TLS 1.3 encryption in transit and AES-256 encryption at rest.
Patient electronic medical health data exports must conform structurally with HL7/FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) schemas to support external clinical data interoperability.
To prevent fraud and maintain strict medical history, medical EHR records, progress notes, and system audit trail database entries cannot be permanently hard-deleted by any application role.

non_functional_requirements.description: API response latency must be minimal under standard operation.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: 95% of API transactions must complete inside 200 milliseconds under load spikes up to 500 concurrent users.

non_functional_requirements.description: System must guarantee nearly continuous availability for emergency room activities.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: Achieve 99.95% annual uptime (excluding pre-scheduled administrative maintenance downtime windows outside core clinical hours).

non_functional_requirements.description: Protection of active sessions from clinical workstations.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: The web portal must log out and terminate the token on the client interface within 10 seconds of closing the active tab or after 15 minutes of idle time.

non_functional_requirements.description: The system must enforce authenticated access, role-based authorization, secure session handling, and protection for sensitive data.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: 100% of protected pages and APIs require authorization checks before access.

non_functional_requirements.description: The system must record security-sensitive actions, administrative changes, and important business events.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_target: Critical user and admin actions are logged with actor, timestamp, action, and target record.
